channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iran's Supreme Leader Vows Revenge After Father's Assassination Amid US-Israeli Tensions
item.description: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei has vowed revenge for the US-Israeli killing of his father, Ali Khamenei, who was assassinated on the first day of the war between Iran and the US-Isra...

item.title: Actors Chanda Romero and Efren Reyes Jr Reflect on Collaborating with Coco Martin in 'Sigabo'
item.description: The article discusses Chanda Romero and Efren Reyes Jr's experience working with Coco Martin on the new project 'Sigabo'. Published on July 12, 2026, the piece highlights their collaboration and insig...

item.title: Mike Sonko Commends US Charity's Medical Equipment Donation to Enhance Kenya's Healthcare Services
item.description: Mike Sonko, National Economic Development Party leader, has praised the US-based humanitarian organisation Care for a Child’s Heart (CFACH) for donating two containers of lifesaving medical equipment ...

item.title: OpenAI expands focus to families with new ChatGPT initiatives
item.description: More than three years after ChatGPT's launch, OpenAI is shifting its focus from individual users to families, caregivers, and older adults. The company is hiring a dedicated product manager in San Fra...

item.title: Spain advances to World Cup semi-finals in thrilling clash against Belgium
item.description: Spain secured their place in the World Cup semi-finals with a hard-fought 2-1 victory over Belgium in a pulsating match at Los Angeles Stadium. Despite Belgium's aggressive tactics and a late goal fro...

item.title: TOMORA's Music Sounds the World Cup: A Fusion of Football and Art
item.description: TOMORA, the collaborative project between Norwegian art-pop artist Aurora and The Chemical Brothers' Tom Rowlands, have shared insights about their music being featured in the UK's World Cup TV covera...

item.title: Noskova clinches Wimbledon women’s singles title in thrilling final against Muchova
item.description: Linda Noskova, 21, secured her first Grand Slam title by overcoming fellow Czech Karolina Muchova in a dramatic Wimbledon women’s singles final on July 11, 2026. The match, the first Grand Slam single...

item.title: Republican Congressman Greg Murphy Faces Criticism for Supporting Israeli Settlers Against Democratic Colleague
item.description: U.S. Congressman Ro Khanna (D-CA) revealed he was detained by armed Israeli settlers and military personnel during a visit to the West Bank, with Israeli forces allegedly siding with the settlers. MAG...

item.title: Trump's USDA funding cuts and DEI policies strain Iowa farmers' livelihoods
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has faced criticism from Iowa farmers for cutting critical USDA grants tied to diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) programs, exacerbating financial struggles...

item.title: Senior Ekurhuleni officials face corruption charges over unlawful salary hikes and stalled disciplinary actions
item.description: Four former senior officials from Ekurhuleni have been charged with fraud and corruption over alleged unlawful salary increases, blocked disciplinary proceedings against former deputy chief Julius Mkh...

item.title: Periyar Tiger Reserve Launches Initiative to Control Invasive African Catfish
item.description: A conservation initiative has been launched in the Periyar Tiger Reserve (PTR) to manage invasive African catfish, balancing ecological protection with community livelihoods. The project, involving Ke...

item.title: Biologist Names Sea Slug Species After Cape Verde Keeper
item.description: A new species of sea slug has been discovered and named 'Aldisa vozinha' in honor of Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha, who gained global recognition during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Biologist Jesus Ortea,...

item.title: Sam Hartman and Tyla Ochoa's Alleged Relationship: Unconfirmed Details
item.description: The article explores the speculated relationship between American football quarterback Sam Hartman and former Wake Forest soccer player Tyla Ochoa. Hartman first hinted at dating a Wake Forest soccer ...

item.title: Tour de France shortens stage nine due to heatwave safety measures
item.description: The Tour de France has decided to shorten stage nine by 30 km (18.64 miles) due to extreme heatwave conditions, according to organisers. The original 185.5 km route from Malemort to Ussel will now be ...

item.title: RCMP lay murder charges following fatal collision in Vanderhoof
item.description: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) have laid a murder charge following a severe motor vehicle collision in Vanderhoof, British Columbia. The incident, which occurred on July 11, 2026, involved a...

item.title: UNICEF and BARMM Launch Women and Children Protection Unit in Lanao del Sur
item.description: The Women and Children Protection Unit (WCPU) in Wao, Lanao del Sur, has officially opened, marking a significant step in addressing violence and trauma against women and children in the Bangsamoro re...

item.title: Mad Cool Festival 2026 Day Three: Highlights from Twenty One Pilots, Interpol, and Other Artists
item.description: The third day of the Mad Cool Festival 2026 in Madrid delivered a diverse lineup of performances, capturing the essence of the event's vibrant atmosphere. Highlights included Karen Dió's energetic fem...

item.title: Iran Rejects U.S. Talks Amid Trump's Threats Over Strait of Hormuz Disputes
item.description: Iran has rejected U.S. President Donald Trump's call for continued peace talks without a ceasefire, demanding Washington meet Tehran's conditions for resolving transit issues through the Strait of Hor...

item.title: Child Care Centers Key to Preventing Violence Against Children in Tanzania
item.description: Social welfare advocate Dk Gwajima emphasizes that child care centers are vital in preventing violence against children by providing structured environments for safety, supervision, and professional c...

item.title: India's migration patterns reshaping demographic balance through youth and education
item.description: The article discusses how India's migrant workforce, particularly young and educated individuals, is altering the country's demographic landscape. Researchers from Azim Premji University analyzed data...
